# Let's open the provided uploaded file path
import os, glob, json, textwrap, re

# List files
files = glob.glob('/mnt/data/*')
files

# Read the file content
path = '/mnt/data/file-NGUVXqXkv8ABUpWchuxHUR-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    raw = f.read()
len(raw), raw[:5000]

# Let's parse the raw text and find the spans.

raw_text = raw

# We'll build a dictionary list for candidate spans with type, text and a label to help debugging.
candidates = []

# 1) Main Ayah after aya_start marker
A1 = "أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ رَحِيمٌ"
candidates.append(("Aya", A1, "A1_main_aya"))

# 2) Full Ayah from [2]
A2 = "يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آَمَنُوا اجْتَنِبُوا كَثِيرًا مِنَ الظَّنِّ إِنَّ بَعْضَ الظَّنِّ إِثْمٌ وَلَا تَجَسَّسُوا وَلَا يَغْتَبْ بَعْضُكُمْ بَعْضًا أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتًا فَكَرِهْتُمُوهُ وَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ تَوَّابٌ رَحِيمٌ"
candidates.append(("Aya", A2, "A2_ref2_full_aya"))

# 3) Fragment in [3] with explicit cue "يقول الله:"
A3 = "أَيُحِبُّ أَحَدُكُمْ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ لَحْمَ أَخِيهِ مَيْتاً"
candidates.append(("Aya", A3, "A3_ref3_fragment_aya1"))

# 4) Beginning of verse at [3] start (no cue but likely intended)
A4 = "وَلا يَغْتَبْ بَعْضُكُمْ بَعْضاً"
candidates.append(("Aya", A4, "A4_ref3_fragment_aya2"))

# Hadith phrases from main text
H1 = "أتدرون ما الغيبة"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H1, "H1_main_q1"))
H2 = "هي ذكرك أخاك بما يكره"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H2, "H2_main_def"))
H3 = "أفرأيت لو كان في أخيك ما تقول"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H3, "H3_main_q2"))
H4 = "إن كان فيه ما تقول فقد اغتبته، وإن لم يكن فيه ما تقول فقد بهتّه"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H4, "H4_main_answer"))

# Hadith phrases from [1] Islamweb excerpt
H5 = "أَتَدْرُونَ مَا الْغِيبَةُ"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H5, "H5_ref1_q1_diac"))
H6 = "ذِكْرُكَ أَخَاكَ بِمَا يَكْرَهُ"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H6, "H6_ref1_def_diac"))
H7 = "أَفَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ كَانَ فِي أَخِي مَا أَقُولُ"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H7, "H7_ref1_q2_diac"))

# Additional hadiths from [1] header and question text
H8 = "وَأَهْلُ النَّارِ مَنْ مَلَأَ أُذُنَيْهِ"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H8, "H8_ref1_title1_partial"))
H9 = "إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَلَائِكَةً تَنْطِقُ عَلَى ألسنة"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H9, "H9_ref1_title2_partial"))
H10 = "أهل النار من ملأ الله أذنيه من ثناء الناس شرًّا، وهو يسمع"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H10, "H10_ref1_quote1"))
H11 = "إن لله ملائكة تنطق على ألسنة بني آدم بما في المرء من خير وشر"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H11, "H11_ref1_quote2"))
H12 = "إن الله إذا كره عبدًا، نادى جبريل: إني كرهت فلانًا، فأبغضوه، ثم توضع له البغضاء في الأرض"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H12, "H12_ref1_quote3"))

# Hadith from [3] additional lines
H13 = "أتدرون ما الغيبة"  # repeated string already H1, but will capture all occurrences later
# We'll not duplicate by adding; We'll reuse when searching for occurrences to capture duplicates

# But [3] includes the same "هي ذكرك أخاك بما يكره" again; We'll detect duplicates.

# [3] hadith: "أفرأيت لو كان في أخى ما أقول"
H14 = "أفرأيت لو كان في أخى ما أقول"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H14, "H14_ref3_q_companion"))
# [3] hadith: final answer without comma
H15 = "إن كان فيه ما تقول فقد اغتبته وإن لم يكن فيه ما تقول فقد بهتّه"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H15, "H15_ref3_answer"))

# [3] end: "وهل يكبّ الناس على مناخرهم إلا حصائد ألسنتهم"
H16 = "وهل يكبّ النّاس على مناخرهم إلّا حصائد ألسنتهم"
candidates.append(("Hadeeth", H16, "H16_ref3_hadith_tongue"))

# Now search for each candidate and collect all occurrences.
def find_all(raw, sub):
    # Returns list of (start, end) for exact occurrences of sub
    starts = []
    i = 0
    while True:
        idx = raw.find(sub, i)
        if idx == -1:
            break
        starts.append(idx)
        i = idx + 1
    return [(s, s+len(sub)) for s in starts]

occurrences = []
for typ, sub, label in candidates:
    locs = find_all(raw_text, sub)
    if not locs:
        print("NOT FOUND:", label, sub[:50])
    else:
        for (s,e) in locs:
            occurrences.append((s,e,sub,typ,label))

# Let's sort by position and print a sample
occurrences_sorted = sorted(occurrences, key=lambda x: x[0])
len(occurrences_sorted), occurrences_sorted[:10]

# Let's filter out overlapping shards when one span is wholly contained in a larger span at the same location context.
# We'll remove a span if it is fully contained within another span with the same citation_type and for which there is a start < contained_start and end > contained_end.
occ = occurrences_sorted.copy()

# For readability, let's mark which ones to drop
to_drop = set()
for i, (s1,e1,txt1,typ1,label1) in enumerate(occ):
    for j, (s2,e2,txt2,typ2,label2) in enumerate(occ):
        if i == j:
            continue
        # if [s1,e1] is inside [s2,e2] and same type and not identical spans
        if s2 <= s1 and e1 <= e2 and (s2 < s1 or e1 < e2) and typ1 == typ2:
            # Only drop if it's clearly a subspan due to same citation text chain; We'll generally drop
            to_drop.add(i)

len(to_drop), sorted(list(to_drop))[:10]

final_occ = [occ[i] for i in range(len(occ)) if i not in to_drop]

len(final_occ), final_occ

# Verify raw[start:end] equals span_text, and gather to output structure
output = []
for (s,e,txt,typ,label) in final_occ:
    assert raw_text[s:e] == txt, (label, s, e, raw_text[s:e], txt)
    output.append({"span_start": s, "span_end": e, "span_text": txt, "citation_type": "Aya" if typ=="Aya" else "Hadeeth"})
len(output)
